name: xax
documentation:
# xax

JAX library for fast experimentation.

## Installation

```bash
pip install xax
```

modules.name: xax.task.launchers.single_process
documentation: Defines a launcher to train a model locally, in a single process.

modules.name: xax.task.launchers.cli
documentation: Defines a launcher that can be toggled from the command line.

modules.name: xax.task.launchers.base
documentation: Defines the base launcher class.

classes.name: xax.task.launchers.base.BaseLauncher

methods.name: xax.task.launchers.base.BaseLauncher.launch
documentation:
Launches the training process.

Args:
    task: The task class to train
    cfgs: The raw configuration to use for training
    use_cli: Whether to include CLI arguments in the configuration
Defines the base launcher class.

modules.name: xax.task.launchers.staged
documentation: Defines a base class with utility functions for staged training runs.

modules.name: xax.task.mixins.process
documentation: Defines a base trainer mixin for handling subprocess monitoring jobs.

classes.name: xax.task.mixins.process.ProcessMixin
documentation: Defines a base trainer mixin for handling monitoring processes.

modules.name: xax
documentation:
Defines the top-level xax API.

This package is structured so that all the important stuff can be accessed
without having to dig around through the internals. This is done by lazily
importing the module by name.

This file can be maintained by running the update script:

```bash
python -m scripts.update_api --inplace
```

modules.name: xax.task.mixins.runnable
documentation: Defines a mixin which provides a "run" method.

classes.name: xax.task.mixins.runnable.RunnableMixin

methods.name: xax.task.mixins.runnable.RunnableMixin.run
documentation: Runs the task.
Mixin which provides a "run" method.

modules.name: xax.task.mixins
documentation: Defines a single interface for all the mixins.

modules.name: xax.task.mixins.logger
documentation: Defines a mixin for incorporating some logging functionality.

modules.name: xax.task.mixins.checkpointing
documentation: Defines a mixin for handling model checkpointing.

functions.name: xax.task.mixins.checkpointing.get_ckpt_path
documentation:
Defines the path to the checkpoint for a given state.

Args:
    exp_dir: The experiment directory
    state: The current trainer state

Returns:
    The path to the checkpoint file.

returns.name:
(
    tuple[PyTree, optax.GradientTransformation, optax.OptState, State, DictConfig]
    | PyTree
    | optax.GradientTransformation
    | optax.OptState
    | State
    | DictConfig
)

modules.name: xax.task.mixins.cpu_stats
documentation:
A trainer mixin for logging CPU statistics.

This logs memory and CPU utilization in a background process, sending it to
the logging process every now and then. This is useful for detecting memory
leaks in your dataloader, among other issues.

classes.name: xax.task.mixins.cpu_stats.CPUStatsMixin
documentation: Defines a task mixin for getting CPU statistics.

modules.name: xax.task.mixins.data_loader
documentation: Defines a mixin for instantiating dataloaders.

methods.name: xax.task.mixins.data_loader.DataloadersMixin.get_dataset
documentation:
Returns the dataset for the given phase.

Args:
    phase: The phase for the dataset to return.

Returns:
    The dataset for the given phase.

modules.name: xax.task.mixins.artifacts
documentation: Defines a mixin for storing any task artifacts.

modules.name: xax.task.logger
documentation:
Defines the core logger.

A common problem when quickly prototyping ML models is nicely logging images,
videos, audio, or other data. Additionally, logging on every step can be
overwhelming. This logger implements a number of convenience functions to
take heterogeneous input data and put it into a standard format, which can
then be used by downstream loggers to actually log the data. For example, this
logger will automatically tile multiple images into a single image, add
captions to images, and so on.

functions.name: xax.task.logger.standardize_text
documentation:
Standardizes a text string to a list of lines.

Args:
    text: The text to standardize
    max_line_length: If set, truncate lines to this length
    remove_non_ascii: Remove non-ASCII characters if present

Returns:
    The standardized text lines

functions.name: xax.task.logger.make_human_viewable_resolution
documentation:
Resizes image to human-viewable resolution.

Args:
    image: The image to resize, with shape (C, H, W)
    interpolation: Interpolation mode to use for image resizing
    trg_res: The target image resolution; the image will be reshaped to
        have approximately the same area as an image with this resolution

Returns:
    The resized image

functions.name: xax.task.logger.image_with_text
documentation:
Adds a text label to an image.

Args:
    image: The image to label, with shape (C, H, W)
    text: The text label for the image
    max_num_lines: The number of lines of spacing to add to the bottom
        of the image
    line_spacing: The spacing between adjacent lines
    centered: If set, center the text labels, otherwise align to the left

Returns:
    The image with a text label

functions.name: xax.task.logger.tile_images_different_sizes
documentation:
Tiles a list of images into a single image, even if they have different sizes.

Args:
    images: The images to tile.
    sep: The separation between adjacent images.

Returns:
    The tiled image.

functions.name: xax.task.logger.tile_images
documentation:
Tiles a list of images into a single image.

Args:
    images: The images to tile.
    sep: The separation between adjacent images.

Returns:
    The tiled image.

methods.name: xax.task.logger.LoggerImpl.__init__
documentation:
Defines some default behavior for loggers.

Every logger needs to implement the `write` function, which handles
actually writing the logs to wherever they needs to go. The basic
class implements a simple interval-based logging scheme to avoid
writing too many log lines.

Args:

: log_interval_seconds: The interval between successive log lines.

methods.name: xax.task.logger.LoggerImpl.write
documentation:
Handles writing the current log line.

Args:
    line: The line to write.

methods.name: xax.task.logger.LoggerImpl.write_error_summary
documentation:
Handles writing an error summary.

Args:
    error_summary: The error summary to write.

methods.name: xax.task.logger.LoggerImpl.write_error
documentation:
Handles writing an error line.

Args:
    error: The error information to write.

methods.name: xax.task.logger.LoggerImpl.write_status
documentation:
Handles writing a status line.

Args:
    status: The status to write.

methods.name: xax.task.logger.LoggerImpl.write_ping
documentation:
Handles writing a ping line.

Args:
    ping: The ping to write.

methods.name: xax.task.logger.LoggerImpl.log_git_state
documentation:
Logs Git state for the current run.

Args:
    git_state: The Git state, as text blocks.

methods.name: xax.task.logger.LoggerImpl.log_training_code
documentation:
Logs the training script code.

Args:
    training_code: The training script code.

methods.name: xax.task.logger.LoggerImpl.log_config
documentation:
Logs the configuration for the current run.

Args:
    config: The configuration, as a DictConfig.

methods.name: xax.task.logger.LoggerImpl.should_log
documentation:
Function that determines if the logger should log the current step.

Args:
    state: The current step's state.

Returns:
    If the logger should log the current step.

classes.name: xax.task.logger.Logger

methods.name: xax.task.logger.Logger.add_logger
documentation:
Add the logger, so that it gets called when `write` is called.

Args:
    logger: The logger to add.

methods.name: xax.task.logger.Logger.write
documentation:
Writes the current step's logging information.

Args:
    state: The current step's state.

methods.name: xax.task.logger.Logger.log_scalar
documentation:
Logs a scalar value.

Args:
    key: The key being logged
    value: The scalar value being logged
    namespace: An optional logging namespace

methods.name: xax.task.logger.Logger.log_string
documentation:
Logs a string value.

Args:
    key: The key being logged
    value: The string value being logged
    namespace: An optional logging namespace

methods.name: xax.task.logger.Logger.log_image

type.name: Callable[[], np.ndarray | Array | PILImage] | np.ndarray | Array | PILImage

documentation:
Logs an image.

Args:
    key: The key being logged
    value: The image being logged
    namespace: An optional logging namespace
    target_resolution: The target resolution for each image; if None,
        don't resample the images

methods.name: xax.task.logger.Logger.log_labeled_image

type.name: Callable[[], tuple[np.ndarray | Array | PILImage, str]] | tuple[np.ndarray | Array | PILImage, str]

documentation:
Logs an image with a label.

Args:
    key: The key being logged
    value: The image and label being logged
    namespace: An optional logging namespace
    max_line_length: The maximum line length for the label
    max_num_lines: The number of lines of spacing to add to the bottom
        of the image
    target_resolution: The target resolution for each image; if None,
        don't resample the images
    line_spacing: The spacing between adjacent lines
    centered: If set, center the text labels, otherwise align to the left

methods.name: xax.task.logger.Logger.log_images

type.name:
(
        Callable[[], Sequence[np.ndarray | Array | PILImage] | np.ndarray | Array]
        | Sequence[np.ndarray | Array | PILImage]
        | np.ndarray
        | Array
    )

documentation:
Logs a set of images.

The images are tiled to be nearly-square.

Args:
    key: The key being logged
    value: The images being logged
    namespace: An optional logging namespace
    max_images: The maximum number of images to show; extra images
        are clipped
    target_resolution: The target resolution for each image; if None,
        don't resample the images
    sep: An optional separation amount between adjacent images

methods.name: xax.task.logger.Logger.log_labeled_images

type.name:
(
        Callable[[], tuple[Sequence[np.ndarray | Array | PILImage] | np.ndarray | Array, Sequence[str]]]
        | tuple[Sequence[np.ndarray | Array | PILImage] | np.ndarray | Array, Sequence[str]]
    )

documentation:
Logs a set of images with labels.

The images are tiled to be nearly-square.

Args:
    key: The key being logged
    value: The images and labels being logged
    namespace: An optional logging namespace
    max_images: The maximum number of images to show; extra images
        are clipped
    max_line_length: The maximum line length for the label
    max_num_lines: The number of lines of spacing to add to the bottom
        of the image
    target_resolution: The target resolution for each image; if None,
        don't resample the images
    line_spacing: The spacing between adjacent lines
    centered: If set, center the text labels, otherwise align to the left
    sep: An optional separation amount between adjacent images
Defines an intermediate container which holds values to log somewhere else.

modules.name: xax.task.mixins.train
documentation: Defines a mixin for running the training loop.

methods.name: xax.task.mixins.train.TrainMixin.log_train_step
documentation:
Override this function to do logging during the training phase.

This function is called after the model forward pass and before the
backward pass. It is called in the training phase.

Args:
    model: The current model.
    batch: The batch from the dataloader.
    output: The model output.
    state: The current training state.

methods.name: xax.task.mixins.train.TrainMixin.log_valid_step
documentation:
Override this function to do logging during the validation phase.

This function is called after the model forward pass. It is called in
the validation phase.

Args:
    model: The current model.
    batch: The batch from the dataloader.
    output: The model output.
    state: The current training state.

methods.name: xax.task.mixins.train.TrainMixin.get_model
documentation:
Returns the Equinox model to train.

Returns:
    The model to train.

methods.name: xax.task.mixins.train.TrainMixin.get_optimizer
documentation:
Gets the optimizer for the model.

Returns:
    The optimizer to use to train the model.

methods.name: xax.task.mixins.train.TrainMixin.get_output
documentation:
Gets the output from the model.

By default, we assume the model is a function that takes the batch as
input and returns the loss. This function can be patched to do more
complex operations instead.

Args:
    model: The current model.
    batch: The current minibatch of samples.
    state: The current training state.

methods.name: xax.task.mixins.train.TrainMixin.compute_loss
documentation:
Gets the loss for the current batch.

By default, we assume the model is a function that takes the batch as
input and returns the loss. This function can be patched to do more
complex operations instead.

Args:
    model: The current model.
    batch: The current minibatch of samples.
    output: The output from the model.
    state: The current training state.

Returns:
    The computed loss, as a tensor.

methods.name: xax.task.mixins.train.TrainMixin.get_size_of_batch
documentation:
Gets the batch size for the current batch.

Args:
    batch: The current minibatch of samples.

Returns:
    The parsed batch size, or None if the batch size could not be
    determined.

methods.name: xax.task.mixins.train.TrainMixin.run_training_loop
documentation:
Runs the training loop.

Args:
    model: The current model
    task: The current task
    optimizer: The current optimizer
    lr_scheduler: The current learning rate scheduler

Raises:
    ValueError: If the task is not a supervised learning task

modules.name: xax.task.mixins.gpu_stats
documentation:
A task mixin for logging GPU statistics.

This logs GPU memory and utilization in a background process using
`nvidia-smi`, if a GPU is available in the system.

classes.name: xax.task.mixins.gpu_stats.GPUStatsMixin
documentation: Defines a task mixin for getting GPU statistics.

modules.name: xax.task.mixins.step_wrapper
documentation: Defines a mixin to wrap some steps in a context manager.

classes.name: xax.task.mixins.step_wrapper.StepContext
documentation: Context manager to get the current step type.

modules.name: xax.task.task
documentation: Composes the base task with all the mixins into a single task interface.

modules.name: xax.task.loggers.state
documentation: Defines a logger which logs the current training state.

modules.name: xax.task.base
documentation:
Defines the base task interface.

This interface is built upon by a large number of other interfaces which
compose various functionality into a single cohesive unit. The base task
just stores the configuration and provides hooks which are overridden by
upstream classes.

methods.name: xax.task.base.BaseTask.get_config_class
documentation:
Recursively retrieves the config class from the generic type.

Returns:
    The parsed config class.

Raises:
    ValueError: If the config class cannot be found, usually meaning
    that the generic class has not been used correctly.

methods.name: xax.task.base.BaseTask.get_config
documentation:
Builds the structured config from the provided config classes.

Args:
    cfgs: The config classes to merge. If a string or Path is provided,
        it will be loaded as a YAML file.
    use_cli: Whether to allow additional overrides from the CLI.

Returns:
    The merged configs.

methods.name: xax.task.base.BaseTask.get_task
documentation:
Builds the task from the provided config classes.

Args:
    cfgs: The config classes to merge. If a string or Path is provided,
        it will be loaded as a YAML file.
    use_cli: Whether to allow additional overrides from the CLI.

Returns:
    The task.

modules.name: xax.task.loggers.json
documentation: Defines a logger which logs JSON lines to a file.

methods.name: xax.task.loggers.json.JsonLogger.__init__
documentation:
Defines a simpler logger which logs to stdout.

Args:
    log_stream: The stream to log to.
    err_log_stream: The stream to log errors to.
    flush_immediately: Whether to flush the file after every write.
    open_mode: The file open mode.
    line_sep: The line separator to use.
    remove_unicode_from_namespaces: Whether to remove unicode from
        namespaces. This is the typical behavior for namespaces that
        use ASCII art for visibility in other logs, but in the JSON
        log file should be ignored.
    log_interval_seconds: The interval between successive log lines.

modules.name: xax.task.loggers.stdout
documentation: Defines a logger that logs to stdout.

methods.name: xax.task.loggers.stdout.StdoutLogger.__init__
documentation:
Defines a logger which shows a pop-up using Curses.

Args:
    write_fp: The file to write logs to.
    precision: The integer precision to use when logging scalars.
    log_timers: Whether to log timers.
    log_perf: Whether to log performance metrics.
    log_optim: Whether to log optimizer parameters.
    log_fp: Whether to log floating point parameters.
    log_interval_seconds: The interval between successive log lines.
    remove_temporary_after: The time after which temporary toasts
        are removed.

modules.name: xax.task.script
documentation: Composes various mixins into a single script interface.

modules.name: xax.task.loggers.callback
documentation: Defines a logger that calls a callback function with the log line.

modules.name: xax.task.loggers.tensorboard
documentation: Defines a Tensorboard logger backend.

methods.name: xax.task.loggers.tensorboard.TensorboardLogger.__init__
documentation:
Defines a logger which writes to Tensorboard.

Args:
    run_directory: The root run directory.
    subdirectory: The subdirectory of the run directory to write
        Tensorboard logs to.
    flush_seconds: How often to flush logs.
    wait_seconds: Time to wait before starting Tensorboard process.
    start_in_subprocess: Start TensorBoard subprocess.
    use_localhost: Use localhost for TensorBoard address.
    log_interval_seconds: The interval between successive log lines.

modules.name: xax.core.state
documentation: Defines a dataclass for keeping track of the current training state.

modules.name: xax.core.conf
documentation: Defines base configuration functions and utilities.

functions.name: xax.core.conf.field
documentation:
Short-hand function for getting a config field.

Args:
    value: The current field's default value.
    kwargs: Additional metadata fields to supply.

Returns:
    The dataclass field.

functions.name: xax.core.conf.is_missing
documentation:
Utility function for checking if a config key is missing.

This is for cases when you are using a raw dataclass rather than an
OmegaConf container but want to treat them the same way.

Args:
    cfg: The config to check
    key: The key to check

Returns:
    Whether or not the key is missing a value in the config

functions.name: xax.core.conf.load_user_config
documentation:
Loads the `~/.xax.yml` configuration file.

Returns:

: The loaded configuration.

modules.name: xax.utils.data.collate
documentation: Defines custom collation functions for PyTorch datasets.

functions.name: xax.utils.data.collate.pad_sequence
documentation:
Pads or truncates a sequence of tensors to the same length.

Args:
    tensors: The tensors to pad or truncate
    dim: The dimension to pad or truncate
    max_length: The maximum tensor length
    left_pad: If set, pad on the left side, otherwise pad the right side
    left_truncate: If set, truncate on the left side, otherwise truncate
        on the right side
    pad_value: The padding value to use

Returns:
    The padded or truncated tensors

Raises:
    ValueError: If the tensor dimensions are invalid

functions.name: xax.utils.data.collate.pad_all
documentation:
Pads all tensors to the same shape.

Args:
    tensors: The tensors to pad
    max_length: The maximum tensor length
    left_pad: If set, pad on the left side, otherwise pad the right side
    left_truncate: If set, truncate on the left side, otherwise truncate
        on the right side
    pad_value: The padding value to use

Returns:
    The padded tensors

functions.name: xax.utils.data.collate.collate
documentation:
Defines a general-purpose collating function.

Args:
    items: The list of items to collate
    mode: Either `stack`, `concat`, or a custom function which is called on
        a list of tensors and returns a single tensor
    pad: If set to True, pads sequences using the default padding function.
        Can also pass a function which will perform padding

Returns:
    The collated item, or None if the item list was empty

Raises:
    NotImplementedError: If the mode is invalid

modules.name: xax.utils.tensorboard
documentation: Defines utility functions for interfacing with Tensorboard.

classes.name: xax.utils.tensorboard.TensorboardWriter
documentation:
Defines a class for writing artifacts to Tensorboard.

Parameters:
    log_directory: The directory to write logs to.
    max_queue_size: The maximum queue size.
    flush_seconds: How often to flush logs.
    filename_suffix: The filename suffix to use.

modules.name: xax.utils.text
documentation: Defines helper functions for displaying text in the terminal.

functions.name: xax.utils.text.render_text_blocks
documentation:
Renders a collection of blocks into a single string.

Args:
    blocks: The blocks to render.
    newline: The string to use as a newline separator.
    align_all_blocks: If set, aligns the widths for all blocks.
    padding: The amount of padding to add to each block.

Returns:
    The rendered blocks.

functions.name: xax.utils.text.format_timedelta
documentation:
Formats a delta time to human-readable format.

Args:
    timedelta: The delta to format
    short: If set, uses a shorter format

Returns:
    The human-readable time delta

functions.name: xax.utils.text.format_datetime
documentation:
Formats a datetime to human-readable format.

Args:
    dt: The datetime to format

Returns:
    The human-readable datetime

modules.name: xax.utils.jax
documentation: Defines some utility functions for interfacing with Jax.

modules.name: xax.utils.logging
documentation: Logging utilities.

functions.name: xax.utils.logging.configure_logging
documentation:
Instantiates logging.

This captures logs and reroutes them to the Toasts module, which is
pretty similar to Python logging except that the API is a lot easier to
interact with.

Args:
    prefix: An optional prefix to add to the logger
    rank: The current rank, or None if not using multiprocessing
    world_size: The total world size, or None if not using multiprocessing

functions.name: xax.utils.logging.get_unused_port
documentation:
Returns an unused port number on the local machine.

Args:
    default: A default port to try before trying other ports.

Returns:
    A port number which is currently unused

functions.name: xax.utils.logging.port_is_busy
documentation:
Checks whether a port is busy.

Args:
    port: The port to check.

Returns:
    Whether the port is busy.

methods.name: xax.utils.logging.RankFilter.__init__
documentation:
Logging filter which filters out INFO logs on non-zero ranks.

Args:
    rank: The current rank

classes.name: xax.utils.logging.ColoredFormatter
documentation: Defines a custom formatter for displaying logs.

modules.name: xax.utils.numpy
documentation: Defines some Numpy utility functions.

functions.name: xax.utils.numpy.partial_flatten
documentation:
Flattens all but the first dimension of an array.

Args:
    x: The array to flatten.

Returns:
    The flattened array.

functions.name: xax.utils.numpy.one_hot
documentation:
Converts an array of labels to a one-hot representation.

Args:
    x: The array of labels.
    k: The number of classes.
    dtype: The dtype of the returned array.

Returns:
    The one-hot representation of the labels.

functions.name: xax.utils.numpy.worker_chunk
documentation:
Chunks an array into `num_workers` chunks.

Args:
    x: The array to chunk.
    worker_id: The worker ID.
    num_workers: The number of workers.
    dim: The dimension to chunk along.

Returns:
    The chunked array.

modules.name: xax.nn.functions
documentation: Defines helper Torch functions.
mypy: disable-error-code="override"

functions.name: xax.nn.functions.recursive_apply
documentation:
Applies a function recursively to tensors in an item.

Args:
    item: The item to apply the function to
    func: The function to apply (for the tensor)
    include_numpy: If set, include numpy arrays

Returns:
    The same item, with the function applied

functions.name: xax.nn.functions.recursive_chunk
documentation:
Recursively chunk tensors N times.

Args:

: item: The item to recursively chunk
  num_chunks: The number of splits to make
  dim: The split dimension

Yields:

: `num_chunks` chunks of items

modules.name: xax.nn.parallel
documentation: Defines functions for parallelism.

modules.name: xax.nn.embeddings
documentation: Defines embedding layers.

functions.name: xax.nn.embeddings.rotary_embeddings
documentation:
Defines a single function for applying rotary embeddings.

This is slower than using the module, but it doesn't require
pre-initializing the embeddings, so it can be used when running online.

Args:

: x_tc: The input tensor, with shape `(batch, tsz, embed_dim)`.
  offset: The offset for the first element.
  base: The base for the sinusoidal embeddings.

Returns:

: The input tensor with rotary embeddings applied.

functions.name: xax.nn.embeddings.get_positional_embeddings
documentation:
Defines the common module for adding positional embeddings.

Args:
    kind: The type of embedding to use.
    max_tsz: The maximum sequence length.
    embed_dim: The embedding dimension.
    learnable: Whether the embeddings are learnable; if not provided,
        uses sensible defaults.
    base: The base for the sinusoidal embeddings.
    key: The PRNG key for initializing learnable embeddings.

Returns:
    The positional embeddings module.

Raises:
    ValueError: If an invalid embedding kind is supplied.

classes.name: xax.nn.embeddings.LearnedPositionalEmbeddings
documentation:
Defines a learned embeddings module.

Parameters:
    max_tsz: The maximum sequence length.
    embed_dim: The embedding dimension.
    weight_init: The initialization type for the embedding weight.
    learnable: Whether the embeddings are learnable.

classes.name: xax.nn.embeddings.SinusoidalEmbeddings
documentation:
Defines a sinusoidal embeddings module.

Parameters:
    embed_dim: The embedding dimension.
    max_tsz: The maximum sequence length.
    learnable: Whether the embeddings are learnable.
    base: The base for the sinusoidal embeddings.

classes.name: xax.nn.embeddings.RotaryEmbeddings

methods.name: xax.nn.embeddings.RotaryEmbeddings.__init__
documentation:
Defines a rotary embeddings module.

Args:
    base: The base for the sinusoidal embeddings.

Defines a rotary embeddings module.

Parameters:
    base: The base for the sinusoidal embeddings.

classes.name: xax.nn.embeddings.FourierEmbeddings
documentation:
Defines a module for applying Fourier embeddings to timesteps.

This module differs from the other positional embedding modules because it
expects a continuous time input, rather than a discrete time input.

Parameters:
    dim: The number of embedding dimensions. This value is used to determine
        how many different frequencies to use, and a higher value means
        higher frequencies.
    max_period: The maximum period for the embeddings. This should roughly
        be in line with the maximum number of timesteps; the default value
        of 10,000 is commonly used in NLP applications, and is derived from
        operating on sequence lengths of 100 to 1000 tokens.

modules.name: xax.utils.experiments
documentation: Functions for managing experiments.

functions.name: xax.utils.experiments.diff_configs
documentation:
Returns the difference between two configs.

Args:
    first: The first (original) config
    second: The second (new) config
    prefix: The prefix to check (used for recursion, not main call)

Returns:
    Two lists of lines describing the diff between the two configs

functions.name: xax.utils.experiments.to_markdown_table
documentation:
Converts a config to a markdown table string.

Args:
    config: The config to convert to a table.

Returns:
    The config, formatted as a Markdown string.

functions.name: xax.utils.experiments.stage_environment
documentation:
Stages the current task to a staging directory.

Args:
    obj: The object with the module to stage.
    root: The root directory to stage to.

functions.name: xax.utils.experiments.get_git_state
documentation:
Gets the state of the Git repo that an object is in as a string.

Args:
    obj: The object which is in the target Git repo.
    width: The width of the text blocks.

Returns:
    A nicely-formatted string showing the current task's Git state.

functions.name: xax.utils.experiments.get_training_code
documentation:
Gets the text from the file containing the provided object.

Args:
    obj: The object to get the file from.

Returns:
    The text from the file containing the object.

functions.name: xax.utils.experiments.check_md5
documentation:
Checks the MD5 of the downloaded file.

Args:
    file_path: Path to the downloaded file.
    hash_str: Expected MD5 of the file; if None, return True.
    chunk_size: Size of the chunks to read from the file.

Returns:
    True if the MD5 matches, False otherwise.

functions.name: xax.utils.experiments.check_sha256
documentation:
Checks the SHA256 of the downloaded file.

Args:
    file_path: Path to the downloaded file.
    hash_str: Expected SHA256 of the file; if None, return True.
    chunk_size: Size of the chunks to read from the file.

Returns:
    True if the SHA256 matches, False otherwise.

functions.name: xax.utils.experiments.get_state_dict_prefix
documentation:
Returns the parts of a checkpoint which begin with a prefix.

Args:
    ckpt: The checkpoint to modify
    prefix: The prefix to clip
    suffix: The suffix to clip
    regexp: The regexp to search for (doesn't modify any keys)

Returns:
    The modified checkpoint

functions.name: xax.utils.experiments.split_n_items_across_workers
documentation:
Computes offsets for splitting N items across K workers.

This returns the start and end indices for the items to be processed by the
given worker. The end index is exclusive.

Args:
    n: The number of items to process.
    worker_id: The ID of the current worker.
    num_workers: The total number of workers.

Returns:
    The start and end index for the items in the current worker.

classes.name: xax.utils.experiments.CumulativeTimer
documentation: Defines a simple timer to track an average value.

classes.name: xax.utils.experiments.IterationTimer
documentation: Defines a simple timer to track consecutive values.

classes.name: xax.utils.experiments.StateTimer
documentation: Defines a timer for all state information.

classes.name: xax.utils.experiments.NaNError
documentation: Raised when NaNs are detected in the model parameters.

classes.name: xax.utils.experiments.TrainingFinishedError
documentation: Raised when training is finished.

classes.name: xax.utils.experiments.MinGradScaleError
documentation:
Raised when the minimum gradient scale is reached.

This is a subclass of {class}`TrainingFinishedError` because it indicates
that training is finished and causes the post-training hooks to be run.

classes.name: xax.utils.experiments.BaseFileDownloader

methods.name: xax.utils.experiments.BaseFileDownloader.ensure_downloaded
documentation:
Ensures the file is downloaded and returns the path to it.

By default, we only check the hash once when the file is downloaded,
and we don't bother rechecking unless `recheck_hash` is set to True.

Returns:

: The path to the downloaded file.

Provides a simple interface for downloading URLs.

This class is meant to be subclassed to provide different download
locations. For example, when downloading pretrained models, use the
{class}`ModelDownloader` class.

Typically, you should simply use the {func}`ensure_downloaded` function
to make sure the file is downloaded to the correct location.

This is adapted in large part from the reference implementation in the
Torchvision library.

Parameters:

: url: The URL to download from.
  dnames: The directory names to download to.
  md5: The expected MD5 of the file.
  sha256: The expected SHA256 of the file.
  is_tmp: Whether to download to a temporary directory.
  recheck_hash: Whether to recheck the hash after downloading.
  max_redirect_hops: The maximum number of redirects to follow.
